english: daṅ bka-źib-tu bgrós-pa-la (dang bka' zhib tu bgros pa la) deliberating carefully with the ten ministers of the interior Pth. (Schr. gives also: bka-grós (bka' gros) drí-ba (dri ba) to ask, — byéd-pa (byed pa) to give advice), — bka-ₒgyur (bka' 'gyur) (*ká-gyur*, com. *kan-gyúr, kan-dyúr*, in Mongolia *kan-jur*) the word of Buddha, as translated from the original Sanskrit, the holy scriptures of the Buddhists (100 volumes). — bka ₒgrol-ba (bka' 'grol ba) to dismiss Pth., bka-bkrol (bka' bkrol) leave of absence, ɣsol-ba (gsol ba) to ask for, ɣnaṅ-ba (gnang ba) to grant leave Schr. — bka-rgya (bka' rgya), bka-śog (bka' shog), resp. for rgya-ma (rgya ma) and śog-bu (shog bu), letter or paper from a superior etc, diploma, missive, communication etc. bka-rgyúd (bka' rgyud) 1. = bkai rgyud (bka'i rgyud) 'thread of the word', the oral tradition of the word of Buddha, which is supposed to have been delivered through a continual series of teachers and disciples besides the written scriptures; bka-rgyud bla-ma (bka' rgyud bla ma) a Lama deriving his religious knowledge in this manner from Buddha himself Mil. 2. perh. also = bka daṅ rgyud (bka' dang rgyud) 'word and tantra', oral and written instruction; bka-rgyúd-pa (bka' rgyud pa) n. of a Lama-sect Schl. 73.; bka-bsgos (bka' bsgos) commandment, precept. — bka-sgyúr (bka' sgyur) order, bka-sgyúr ɣnaṅ-ba (bka' sgyur gnang ba) to issue an order C. — bka sgyúr-ba (bka' sgyur ba) 1. to translate the words (of Buddha etc.) 2. to issue an order (viz. in the name of a superior). — bka sgrog-pa (bka' sgrog pa) 1. to publish an order. 2. to proclaim, read, preach the word. — bka ɣćog-pa (bka' gcog pa) to act against an order, yab-kyi bka bćag-tu med (yab kyi bka' bcag tu med) the order of the father must be obeyed Glr. — bka-čéms (bka' chems) resp. for k'a-čéms (kha chems) testament. — bka-mčíd (bka' mchid) resp. for mčíd (mchid), words or speech of a superior or any person to be honoured. — bka nyan-pa (bka' nyan pa) ccyp. 1. vb. to obey. 2. adj. obedient, bka mi nyan-pa (bka' mi nyan pa) 1. to disobey. 2. disobedient. bka ɣnyan (bka' gnyan) 'the cruel commander', acc. to a Lex. = btsan-pai sa-bdag (btsan pa'i sa bdag) 'the mighty lord of the ground', is said to be the first of gods, either Siva or a pre-buddhistic deity. — *ka taṅ-će* (*ka tang che*) W. to permit. — bka btags-pa (bka' btags pa) (Lex.: = krims bsgrags-pa (khrims bsgrags pa)) a proclaimed order, cf. bkar (bkar). — bka-rtags (bka' rtags) Cs.: mark, seal, precept, maxim (?) — bka-stod (bka' stod) Sch.: 'a subaltern, agent' (?) — bka-taṅ (bka' tang) order, edict. — bka-driṅ (bka' drin) resp. for drin (drin), favour, grace, kindness, benefit, bka-drin mdzád-pa (bka' drin mdzad pa) to bestow a favour, mi-la (mi la) upon somebody; blá-mai bka-drín-gyis (bla ma'i bka' drin gyis) through the kindness of his (your) reverence Mil.; bka-drin-čé (bka' drin che) the usual phrase of acknowledgment, as our: you are very kind! many thanks! B. and col. — bka-druṅ (bka' drung) secretary (of a high person) C. — bka-ɣdáms (bka' gdams), = źal-ɣdáms (zhal gdams) advice, counsel, instruction; bka-ɣdáms-pa (bka' gdams pa) 1. adviser Sch. 2. n. of a sect of red Lamas, founded by Brom-ston (brom ston) Schl. 73. — bka-klógs-pa (bka' klogs pa) to proclaim; proclamation. — bka-bsdú-ba (bka' bsdu ba) collection of the doctrine Tar. — *ka-nán* (*ka nan*) instruction C. — bka ɣnáṅ-ba (bka' gnang ba) 1. vb. to order, command; grant, permit; 2. sbst. order; permission; ṅed bód-kyi rgyál-poi btsún-mo-la bka ɣnáṅ-bar źu (nged bod kyi rgyal po'i btsun mo la bka' gnang bar zhu) I beg you will give her as a consort to my (the Tibetan) king Glr. — bka-p̌ébs (bka' phebs) Sch. a supreme order. — bka-p̌rín (bka' phrin) message. — bka ₒbáb-pa (bka' 'bab pa) the going forth of an order, bka-ₒbáb (bka' 'bab) order, edict Schr. — bka-ₒbúm (bka' 'bum), vulg. *kam-búm*, the hundred thousand precepts (title of a book). — bka stsol-ba (bka' stsol ba), pf. stsal (stsal) (stsál-to (stsal to), stsal-pa (stsal pa)), resp. for smrá-ba (smra ba) to speak, to say (acc. to circumstances: to command, ask, beg, relate, answer etc.), esp. in ancient literature, in which it is almost invariably used of Buddha and of kings. — bka-blo-bdé (bka' blo bde) Lex.: = सुवचस् speaking well, eloquently; Sch.: bka-blo-bdé-ba (bka' blo bde ba) to acknowledge to be wrong (?); bka-blón (bka' blon), (bkai blón-po (bka'i blon po) Glr. f. 94) prime minister; any high official. — bka-śóg (bka' shog) any writing of a superior, decree, diploma, passport, official paper, letter. — bka ɣšágs (bka' gshags) 1. a high official, counsellor. C. 2. court of justice, judgment-hall.
term. of bka (bka') in or to the word etc; bkar bdágs-pa (bkar bdags pa) Cs. to legalize, Dzl. cap. 4: to proclaim, publish. bkár-btags-pa (bkar btags pa) published; publication.
